latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED (the "Company")

Share SEDOL:
BYRGPD6
TIDM: ALF

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: ALF.L is a UK‑based investment vehicle that manages a small, diversified portfolio of equities and fixed‑income securities. It operates as a closed‑end trust, buying and selling assets on behalf of its shareholders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The fund sits in the niche of small‑cap, high‑yield investments, targeting investors who seek exposure to undervalued assets that larger funds overlook. Its strategy gives it a unique place in the broader market for alternative returns.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been focusing on liquidity and shareholder returns, issuing capital back to owners and updating its holdings. Its share price is essentially nil, trading at 0.00p, indicating a severe discount to the underlying assets.

rns_narrative: ALF.L has been actively returning capital to shareholders, as shown by the 22 September 2025 return‑of‑capital announcement and the 11 December 2025 AGM results.
The 5 March 2026 Net Asset Value factsheet and the 3 July 2025 portfolio update suggest the fund is refining its holdings and providing more transparency to investors.
Recent filings focus on governance and liquidity rather than new growth initiatives, indicating a period of consolidation.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence with a hint of caution – investors are waiting for a clear direction.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None identified – the small‑cap community is largely indifferent, likely because the fund offers little in the way of headline growth.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The main worry is that the fund may stay trapped at a negligible price if it cannot demonstrate a clear investment thesis or if its assets underperform.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from neutral to slightly negative, with no new positive chatter emerging.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public buzz, giving the stock a high surprise potential if news hits.

near_term_catalysts: A new capital call or injection of fresh funds could signal confidence and provide a liquidity boost.
An announcement of a strategic shift – such as a new sector focus or a change in portfolio weighting – would give a clear direction.
The next quarterly NAV update (or the 5 March 2026 factsheet) could reveal improved underlying asset values, encouraging a price rebound.
A volume spike that breaks the 50‑day moving average would indicate renewed interest and could trigger a bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for: 1) any new RNS that outlines a new investment strategy; 2) a sudden jump in daily traded shares; 3) a change in the fund’s holdings that signals a move away from low‑yield assets.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash – it has not yet recovered and no prior bounce signals exist. Confirmation of mean reversion would likely come from a clear strategic announcement or a volume surge.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Speculative Long – the depth of the discount offers upside potential, but the lack of prior bounce signals and unclear fundamentals keep the risk high.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the fund’s NAV rises to a level that matches half of its previous high (roughly 50p if the prior high were 100p), the upside would be around 5,000 % from the current price. A single strategic announcement that improves the asset mix could unlock this potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The fund could remain trapped at a negligible price if it fails to demonstrate a clear investment thesis or if its underlying assets continue to underperform. A regulatory filing that raises concerns about the fund’s structure could also push the price back to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: total loss of capital – the stock is already at 0.00p. Reward: potentially thousands of percent if it reaches a mid‑level recovery. The asymmetry is extreme – a small event could create a huge upside, but a single negative signal could keep the stock flat.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a small, clear volume bounce above 0.01p, size 5 % of portfolio, place a stop just below that bounce (e.g., 0.005p). Use a trailing stop that protects the upside while allowing for the stock’s volatility.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a new RNS announces a strategic shift or a capital call is made. A volume spike of >10 % above the 20‑day average would also justify a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. The only terminal risk would be the fund staying at zero.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ALF.L is a deeply discounted, low‑profile fund that could surprise the market if a clear strategy or capital injection materialises, but it carries a high risk of remaining stuck at zero.
